# quick start

# What is the Sungrow Developer Portal?

![](data:image/png;base64,)

**Sungrow Developer Portal** is an open API service portal built by Sungrow for global developers, system integrators and third-party enterprises.

    Through this platform, you can securely and efficiently access operational data from smart devices such as PV inverters and energy storage systems, and deeply integrate them into your own business ecosystem.

    Whether you are building a remote operation and maintenance and device management system for distributors, or developing a Virtual Power Plant (VPP) integration platform for power grid dispatching.

    the Developer Portal provides you with standardized API interfaces, comprehensive development documentation, test sandboxes and application management tools, enabling one-stop secondary development and innovation for energy digitalization.

# Register for a Developer Portal Account

**Login and Registration Guide for Developer Portal**

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Please select the corresponding access method based on your business identity:

- **Existing iSolarCloud installers: > No registration required**. If you need to monitor and control new energy devices under your account via API, you may log in to this Developer Portal directly using your existing iSolarCloud account.

- **Third-party monitoring platforms and VPP service providers: > Registration required.** Please register and complete the developer account verification process as a new enterprise or individual developer on the platform to obtain exclusive API access permissions.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

For third-party monitoring platforms and VPP service providers, please complete the registration and certification of developer accounts in accordance with the following steps.

- **Step 1: Select a Service Site** > On the registration page, in the Select Site drop-down menu, choose the corresponding service region according to your business location (e.g., China Site, Europe Site, Australia Site, International Site).

         Note:  The selected site must be consistent with the server region to which your devices are physically connected.

- **Step2: Enter Email and Verify** > Input a valid email address and click **send** to obtain a verification code. Enter the 6-digit numeric verification code received in your email into the **verification code** field.
- **Step3: Set a Secure Password** > Set your login password and enter it again in Confirm Password. Please ensure the password meets complexity requirements(it is recommended to include uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and special symbols).
- **Step4: complete Entity Information** >     Country/Region: Select the country where your enterprise is registered or where you reside.

           Company Name: Enter the full legal name of the enterprise accurately.

- **Step5: Sign the Agreement and Submit** > Read and check I have read and agree to the Service Agreement and Privacy Policy. Click the Confirm button to submit your registration application.

# Development of Learning Map Based on User-Level  API

![](data:image/png;base64,)

## Application and Credential Acquisition

![](data:image/png;base64,)

 Before starting API integration, you need to first apply for an application on the developer platform to obtain valid communication credentials.

     1. **Enter the Application Management interface**

       After logging in to the Developer Portal, click the **Applications** icon in the upper-right corner of the top navigation bar to enter your personal Application Center.

     2. **Improve Basic Information**

**Application Name (Required)**: Provide a distinctive name to facilitate the management of multiple applications later.

**Company Name (Required)**: Enter your company name.

**Application Description (Required)**: Briefly describe the purpose of the application. A clear description helps speed up the review process.

**Upload Application Avatar (Optional)**: Upload an icon for your application to improve business recognition.

     3. **Configure Authorization Mode**

       Key Selection: For the "**User-level authorization**" scenario, please select **No** for the "**Use OAuth 2.0 Authorization**" option.

       After submitting the application, you will be able to find the application information in the console. The application status will be Under Review. You will be notified of the review result within **3–5 working days**.

## Power Station Access Authorization

### Power Station Sharing

  After the application configuration is completed, the third-party platform must obtain valid authorization from the equipment owner and installer before accessing and using relevant data.

**Power Station Sharing Operation Process**

  1、Step1: Trigger Sharing

![](data:image/png;base64,)

    On the iSolarCloud power station list page, locate the target power station, click the share icon on the right, and open the sharing pop-up window.

  2、Step2: Enter the configuration interface

![](data:image/png;base64,)

    In the sharing pop-up window, click [+ Add Share] to enter the sharee configuration page.

  3、Step3: Configure sharing information and submit.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

- Enter the phone number of the share recipient;
- Select sharing permissions (Viewer/Administrator)；
- Tick the declaration **I have obtained authorization from the owner**.
- Click **Confirm** to complete sharing.

### Revoke Power Station Sharing

**Power Station Sharing Revocation Process**

  1、**Step1: Enter the power station sharing interface**.

    On the power station list page, locate the target power station, click the **Share** icon on the right to open the sharing pop-up window.

  2、**Step2: Remove share recipient**

    In the sharing list, click the - button next to the target share recipient. In the confirmation pop-up "**Stop sharing with this user**", click **Confirm** to remove the user's power station sharing permission.

## API Integration and Interaction

### Identity Authentication and Token Issuance

#### Prerequisites

- appkey
- secret Key
- user\_account/user\_password

#### 

#### Login Request

![](data:image/png;base64,)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

1、Call the **/openapi/login** interface.

2、**Request Header**

- Content-Type: application/json
- x-access-key: your application's Secret Key
- sys\_code: 901

3、**Request Body**

- appkey: your appkey
- user\_account: your account
- user\_password: your account password

#### Response Result

```
{
    "req_serial_num": "202411114fa4436f9bc807bc966920ec",
    "result_code": "1",
    "result_msg": "success",
    "result_data": {
        "user_master_org_id": "89899046",
        "mobile_tel": "153****5",
        "user_name": "test user1",
        "language": "Chinese",
        "token": "999125867_91bbb35be9f74ccebb0406307ca80874",
        "err_times": "0",
        "user_id": "1",
        "login_state": "1",
        "disable_time": null,
        "country_name": "China",
        "user_account": "test user1",
        "user_master_org_name": "org-ty1867erni",
        "email": "",
        "country_id": "1"
    }
}
```

- If  **result\_code** is "1", the request is successful.
- Extract the token from **result\_data** as the identity credential for subsequent API calls.

### Business Request and Data Parsing

#### Prerequisites

     A valid token has been obtained through the **/openapi/login** interface.

#### Initiate Request

![](data:image/png;base64,)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

- Call the **/openapi/getPowerStationList** interface. ；

- **Request Header**

  Content-Type: application/json

  x-access-key: your application's Secret Key

  sys\_code: 901

- **Request Body**

        appkey: your appkey

        token: your token

**Process Response**

```
{
  "req_serial_num": "202411124b514f528da409106b0bec7b",
  "result_code": "1",
  "result_msg": "success",
  "result_data": {
    "pageList": [
      {
        "ps_name": "MN20220720A",
        "online_status": 0,
        "latitude": 31.86406773478788,
        "description": null,
        "valid_flag": 1,
        "grid_connection_status": 1,
        "ps_fault_status": 3,
        "ps_location": "H2O UNITY Coffee",
        "update_time": "2024-09-02 17:20:37",
        "ps_current_time_zone": "GMT-2",
        "ps_id": 342459181,
        "grid_connection_time": 20230808152800,
        "connect_type": 1,
        "install_date": "2023-08-08 15:27:58",
        "build_status": 2,
        "ps_type": 4,
        "longitude": 117.27770144878463
      }
    ],
    "rowCount": 1
  }
}
```

- If the **result\_code** is "1", the request is successful.
- Retrieve the power station list data (such as station name, ID, location, status, etc.) from **result\_data.pageList**.

# Learning Map for API Development Based on OAuth 2.0 Authorization

![](data:image/png;base64,)

## Application and Credential Acquisition

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Before starting API integration, you need to first apply for an application on the developer platform to obtain valid communication credentials.

     1. **Enter the Application Management interface**

       After logging in to the Developer Portal, click the **Applications** icon in the upper-right corner of the top navigation bar to enter your personal Application Center.

     2. **Improve Basic Information**

**Application Name (Required)**: Provide a distinctive name to facilitate the management of multiple applications later.

**Company Name (Required)**: Enter your company name.

**Application Description (Required)**: Briefly describe the purpose of the application. A clear description helps speed up the review process.

**Upload Application Avatar (Optional)**: Upload an icon for your application to improve business recognition.

     3. **Configure Authorization Mode**

     Key Selection: For the "OAuth 2.0 Authorization" scenario, select **Yes** for the option **Enable OAuth 2.0 Authorization**.

     Redirect URL (required if obtaining user authorization via OAuth 2.0): Enter the callback address.

## Power Station Access Authorization

### Third-Party Authorization

If you select use OAuth 2.0 authorization in creating an application, you can view the authorization URL on the application details page after the application is made.

Third-party applications can usually integrate this link, or they can directly copy this link and send it to the plant owner.

![](https://isc-file.oss-cn-hangzhou.aliyuncs.com/openapi/quickStart/application_detail_en_us.png)

 After the plant owner initiates an authorization request from the third-party application, the plant owner will access the OAuth service, and the authorization request address is the Authorization URL above with your application ID and Redirect URL. Example request:

```
GET /authorized-app?cloudId=YOUR_ACCOUNT_BELONG_TO_CLOUD&applicationId=YOUR_APPLICATION_ID&redirectUrl=YOUR_REDIRECT_URI
```

**Plant Owner Authorization**

After the user logs in and agrees to authorize, an Authorization Code will be generated. The page will be redirected to the Redirect URL, and the Authorization Code will be spliced after the Redirect URL.

![](https://isc-file.oss-cn-hangzhou.aliyuncs.com/openapi/quickStart/quickStart1_en_us.png)

![](https://isc-file.oss-cn-hangzhou.aliyuncs.com/openapi/quickStart/redirect_url_zh_ch.png)

#### 

#### 

### Third-Party Authorization Revocation

The end user of the iSolarCloud plant can revoke authorization through the iSolarCloud web or app under **Account and Security** > **Authorization Management**.

The Authorization Management page displays a list of authorized applications.

Click the Authorization Details button to enter the corresponding page: Users can revoke authorization from the Authorization Details page.

![](https://isc-file.oss-cn-hangzhou.aliyuncs.com/openapi/quickStart/quickStart2_en_us.png)

## API Integration and Interaction

### Identity Authentication and Token Issuance

 Use the obtained **Authorization Code** to request an access token from the authorization server.

**Sample Request:**

```
POST /openapi/apiManage/token
Content-Type: application/json
x-access-key: your Secret Key

{
  "appkey":"your appkey",
  "grant_type":"authorization_code",
  "code":"authorization code",
  "redirect_uri":"your redirect url"
}
```

 ❕ Note: The authorization code can only be used once. Please ensure that the **appkey**, **redirect\_uri** parameters and the request header  **x-access-key** are correct. Re-authorization will be required upon failure.

After the authorization server verifies the request, it will return an **Access Token** and a **Refresh Token**. You can use the Access Token to access API interfaces.

**Response Example:**

```
{
  "req_serial_num": "202409238d774af79943848e43f64876",
  "result_code": "1",
  "result_msg": "success",
  "result_data": {
    "access_token": "84a25a35-efa4-42b6-b573-9144ae5b923d",
    "token_type": "bearer",
    "refresh_token": "d545a0ab-ff3b-4d1f-b3ef-524fd6f60c6b",
    "expires_in": 172799,
    "auth_ps_list": [
      "1122348203"
    ],
    "auth_user": 65199
  }
}
```

**How to Refresh the Token**

The access\_token has a short validity period (currently 2 days). When the access\_token expires, you can use the refresh\_token (valid for 30 days) to refresh it.

**Request Example:**

```
POST /openapi/apiManage/refreshToken
Content-Type: application/json
x-access-key: your Secret Key

{
    "refresh_token":"f659b573-8c89-4bbb-9ec4-d2d601cb9733",
    "appkey":"074C20C520E3EF193A52D5392FB7C81F"
}
```

**Response Example:**

```
{
  "req_serial_num": "20240923eb9c43218b70129d3284ccaa",
  "result_code": "1",
  "result_msg": "success",
  "result_data": {
    "access_token": "3b1274dd-a944-4c8e-ae09-bc89c20c9242",
    "refresh_token": "a05246f8-e0e5-4be9-a8a5-e9190bcea061",
    "code": "1",
    "token_type": "bearer",
    "expires_in": 172799
  }
}
```

### Business Request and Data Parsing

![](data:image/png;base64,)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

 The developer can request the plant data of an authorized iSolarCloud end user by calling the relevant endpoints of the Developer portal.

When making an endpoint request, add the authorization obtained in the previous step in the HTTP request body. The value is: "Bearer "+ access\_token.
